Allow all users to create and manage API keys (#215)

* Allow all users to create and manage API keys

Move API key management from admin-only to user settings. All authenticated users can now create, view, and revoke their own API keys. Backend routes moved from /admin/api-keys to /me/api-keys, UI moved from dedicated admin page to Settings. Admin checks removed since the service layer already scopes keys per creator.

* Extract API keys UI into ApiKeysSection component

Address review feedback: extract the API key management UI (table, create/revoke/display modals) from Settings.svelte into a dedicated ApiKeysSection component. Also suppress 401 error toasts during loadApiKeys to avoid flashing errors when unauthenticated users hit /settings before the redirect to /login fires.

* Update frontend/src/routes.ts

Signed-off-by: Patrick Veverka <veverkap@users.noreply.github.com>

The handler file is no longer admin-specific since routes moved to /me. Update the filename and docs reference to match.

The "/admin/api-keys": "/settings" entry used a string value instead of a
Svelte component, which broke svelte-spa-router entirely and caused all
pages to fail to render. Simply remove the route since the admin API keys
page no longer exists.

* Update screenshot script for API keys in Settings

Remove the admin/api-keys entry from the admin pages list since that page
no longer exists. Add screenshots of the API Keys section within the
Settings page instead.

* Update docs/architecture.md

commit.message:
Add PR comment notifications with deduplication

Created a new notification channel that posts comments directly on PRs
about conflicts, with built-in duplicate detection.

Features:
- ENABLE_PR_COMMENTS env var to opt-in (default: false)
- Posts comment on both PRs in a conflict pair
- Checks for existing comments before posting (signature + PR number)
- Integrates with deduplication (only comments on new/changed conflicts)
- Respects DRY_RUN mode (logs what would be posted)
- Comment includes: conflicting files, line ranges, other PR details

Comment format:
- Clear warning header with bot signature
- Link to conflicting PR with title
- List of files with overlapping line ranges
- Actionable guidance for developers
- Attribution to pr-conflict-detector

Duplicate prevention:
- Before posting, checks if comment already exists for this conflict pair
- If signature + other PR number found in existing comments, skips posting
- Error-safe: if check fails, assumes no comment to avoid blocking

Test coverage: 190 tests passing, 99% overall coverage, 9.92/10 pylint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix RPC starvation: move block processing to spawn_blocking threads

Root cause: synchronous sled I/O inside add_block() (save, read-back
verify, scan_forward, undo_log, height update) runs directly on tokio
worker threads. During batch sync, 400+ sled operations per 50-block
batch saturate all 4 worker threads, starving the RPC accept loop,
timers, and networking — causing time-cli and dashboard to hang.

Changes:
- handle_blocks_response: wrap add_block_with_fork_handling in
  spawn_blocking + Handle::block_on so sled I/O runs on the blocking
  thread pool instead of worker threads
- server.rs: same spawn_blocking treatment for BlockResponse and
  BlockAnnouncement handlers
- add_block: skip post-save read-back hash verification during sync
  (halves sled I/O per block; verification still runs for non-sync blocks)
- add_block: skip scan_forward loop during sync (blocks arrive
  sequentially, no gaps to fill)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial plan (#9)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and

fix: DateTime UTC-Kind-Fehler bei Zeitzonenkonvertierung behoben

Fehler: 'The conversion could not be completed because the supplied
DateTime did not have the Kind property set correctly.'

Ursache:
  System.Text.Json setzt beim Deserialisieren von ISO-8601-Strings mit
  Offset +00:00 je nach Plattform DateTimeKind.Unspecified statt
  DateTimeKind.Utc. TimeZoneInfo.ConvertTimeFromUtc() erwartet jedoch
  zwingend DateTimeKind.Utc und wirft sonst ArgumentException.

Lösung:
  SolarData.cs:
  - Neue private Hilfsmethode AsUtc(DateTime) eingefügt:
    prüft Kind und ruft ggf. DateTime.SpecifyKind(..., Utc) auf
  - GetLocalSunrise() / GetLocalSunset() nutzen nun AsUtc()
  - Neue public-Methode GetLocalSolarNoon(TimeZoneInfo) hinzugefügt,
    die ebenfalls AsUtc() verwendet

  Aufrufer (direktes ConvertTimeFromUtc entfernt):
  - MainForm.cs: data.SolarNoon → data.GetLocalSolarNoon(tz)
  - WallpaperGeneratorService.cs (2×): solarData.SolarNoon →
    solarData.GetLocalSolarNoon(timeZone)

commit.message:
fix(web): fix Sanity webhook signature to match Stripe-style format

Sanity uses Stripe-style signatures: t=<timestamp>,v1=<base64url-hmac>
where the signing payload is `${timestamp}.${body}`. Our original
implementation used plain HMAC-SHA256 hex which would never validate.

Also fixed: import locales from i18n/config instead of hardcoding.
